title: Engineer a latent degradation state with explicit pit-reset dynamics
summary: Recent F1 research models tyre wear as a latent state with pit-stop resets and shows better predictive behavior than simpler autoregressive baselines.
rationale: Recent F1 research models tyre wear as a latent state with pit-stop resets and shows better predictive behavior than simpler autoregressive baselines. That matches this task better than using only raw `Cumulative_Degradation` or short lags.
implementation_hint: Create online-only state features per driver-race sequence: EWMA lap-time residual versus race-compound median, residual slope/curvature over the last 3-5 laps, residual since stint start, and a reset flag after pit/stint change. Exclude pit laps and extreme outliers when updating the state.
expected_effect: Should help distinguish genuinely collapsing tyres from long but still stable stints, improving rank around pit windows.
risk: Lap times are confounded by traffic, weather, and pit in/out laps; poor residualization can add noise instead of signal.
